data.description:
# P1.1 â€” Structured query: field predicates, OR, negation, --sort

## Context (problem + research)

rebar `search` is whitespace-split, case-insensitive **AND of substrings** with a
few fixed flags (`reducer/search.py:search_states(states, query, *, status,
ticket_type, has_tag)`, confirmed by EXP-R10). No OR, no field-scoped predicates
(`assignee:`, `priority:<2`), no caller-controlled sort, no saved queries. Agents
post-filter in their own code, fragmenting behavior. Comparable trackers expose
richer query: Fossil offers SQL report queries; git-bug a `status:open label:x
sort:` query language. This is a read-only, low-risk, high-daily-value upgrade
(I3-clean â€” no writes).

## Evidence (experiments)

- EXP8: a ~40-line parser does field predicates + `OR` + negation + degrade-to-
  substring (unknown `field:` -> literal substring, no crash).
- EXP-R10 (real code): confirmed the real `search_states` and
  `apply_ticket_filters(results, *, type_filter, status_filter, parent_filter,
  tag_filter, priority_filter, without_*...)` signatures â€” the integration seams.

## What / Implementation detail

1. New `reducer/_query.py` parser: tokenize; recognize `field:value`
   (`status:`, `type:`, `priority:` incl. `<`/`>`, `assignee:`, `tag:`,
   `parent:`), leading `-`/`not:` negation, and `OR` between AND-groups; free text
   still matches the existing `_haystack`. Unknown `field:` -> literal substring.
2. Expose one `match_predicate(state, field, op, value)` in `reducer/_filters.py`
   shared by `_query.py` and `apply_ticket_filters` (one comparison vocabulary).
3. `--sort/-s` in `_engine_support/reads.py` + `_reads.py`: keys
   `priority|created|updated|id|status`, `-key` descending; default sort unchanged
   so goldens hold. **Do not** touch `reducer/_sort.py` (that is event replay order,
   not presentation sort).
4. Parity across CLI (`--sort`, help texts), library (`sort=` kwarg), MCP
   (`sort` + bumped inputSchema).

## Success Criteria

- `search "status:open priority:<2 assignee:alice"`, `... OR ...`, and `-status:closed`
  all work; `--sort priority` / `-updated` order results deterministically.
- A plain `search "login"` returns byte-identical results to today (back-compat).
- No event-schema change; no SCHEMA_VERSION bump.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `reducer/_query.py` parser (predicates, OR, negation, substring fallback) + unit tests
- [ ] shared `match_predicate` in `_filters.py` used by both query and filters
- [ ] `--sort` on list/search/ready (CLI), `sort=` (library), `sort` (MCP inputSchema)
- [ ] parity test across all three interfaces + a sorted-list golden
- [ ] back-compat: plain substring search byte-identical to current output

See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.1).
data.title: P1.1 â€” Structured query: field predicates, OR, negation, --sort
